title: Hire through the Rural and Francophone Community Immigration Pilots: Resources for designated employers - Canada.ca
content:
Hire through the Rural and Francophone Community Immigration pilots

## Resources for employers
As a designated employer, we have extra resources to help you succeed.

## Get support from our Dedicated Service Channel
The Dedicated Service Channel (DSC) is a team from IRCC that can help you
- find immigration solutions that fit your employment needs and business plans
- answer questions about the Rural and Francophone Community Immigration pilots and other IRCC programs
- guide candidates through the application process
Your community will refer you to the DSC after you’ve been designated .
- process applications
- expedite applications
- offer special exemptions from the labour market impact assessment process

## How to help newcomer employees settle in your community
There are many ways you can support and retain your newcomer employees.

### Give support in their community
In addition to helping newcomers integrate by connecting them with a Service Provider Organization (SPO) , you can
- help them find housing and feel at home
- help them with transportation pick them up from the airport help them get to work, a local SPO and other essential services give them public transit information
- pick them up from the airport
- help them get to work, a local SPO and other essential services
- give them public transit information
- refer them to key services in the community (i.e. health card and school registration)
- connect them with local groups of interest

### Give support in the workplace
In addition to taking Intercultural Competency Training yourself, you can
- encourage your staff to take the training
- help your new employee build social connections through mentoring and team building
- establish a workplace diversity and inclusion committee

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
